attacking_style: Pure bunch sprinter who thrives in chaotic finales rather than organized trains. Excels in the final 200m with powerful late acceleration, particularly when leadout structures break down.
team_tactics: Functions as backup sprinter option, given freedom when primary sprinter unavailable or faltering. DSM allows opportunistic approaches rather than building dedicated trains around him.
terrain_preference: Flat to moderately flat sprint finishes. Struggles on pure power/flat drags but excels when positioning and timing matter more than raw wattage. Circuit finales expose positioning weaknesses.
vulnerability: Positional awareness in technical circuit finales (50th when favored). Cannot match pure power sprinters like Groves on flat drags. Lacks dedicated leadout support consistently.
key_patterns: Wins as backup option when team structure fails or primary sprinter unavailable (2 instances)
Superior final 200m reading ability versus established sprinters in disorganized finishes (3 observations)
Performs better in chaotic sprints than organized leadout trains (3 instances)
Uses low Cavendish-style position for aerodynamic advantage in final meters (2 observations)
Positional errors cost results in technical finales despite sprint speed (2 negative signals)
form_trajectory: positive: evolved from backup to occasional winner 2020-2023, podiuming even on leadout duty by 2023
